title: forestiera polyandra
intro: Forestiera polyandra, also known as Mexican elder, is a small shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the family Oleaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Forestiera polyandra include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various forms of herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
